Ampitheatre.summary: In the city proper, a space for ceremonies, public events and proclamations.

Ankomeido.summary: Home to the misfits and malcontents of Misteria.

Auis Scale Strongholds.summary: Locations where things are concealed from the public.

Blackjacks Tavern.summary: Bounty Hunting Hub, owned by Greenbird. Neutral Ground.

Blockhead Territory.summary: An entire sector of The Maw.

Candlelight Clearing.summary: Where the Rosetta of old had once gathered to garden, share poetry, and sing.

Ceremionial Chamber.summary: In the city proper, location of the Awakening ceremony.

Coralysi.summary: Home of the merfolk. Floating gardens.

Demonastery.summary: Mansion with specific rooms per resident; it moves, accessible by invitation.

East Rise.summary: The majority of this Metrix sector is devoted to parks and entertainment complexes.

Eighth Precinct.summary: Enforcer station, down the street from the Iron Hall.

Enion.summary: Armory of the ancients and training ground of champions.

Entrance Hall.summary: Location of the portal to íArathael, home of Whisper the stained glass window.

Fedhari.summary: Former Dhani city, now home to the merfolk and named Coralysi

Forward Camps.summary: Home to merchants, traders, adventurers, and mercenaries.

Fractal Scar.summary: Site of the Battle of Fractal Scar.

Freakshow Territory.summary: Deep in the Pits, composed of abandoned mineshafts.

Golden Chariot.summary: Inn in the city proper, owned by Minerva.

Golden Port.summary: Built in the ruins of the Dhani Empire.

Gougemoor.summary: The edge of the Savage Lands.

Ikaru.summary: One of the prestigious houses of Misteria; the House of Blossoms.

Iron Hall.summary: The seat of power for Metrix's municipal government.

Jawbreaker Territory.summary: No official territory with the exception of some houses on the water.

Library of Illumination.summary: In the city proper, a public library cared for by the members of the Light of Sol.

Might n Mead.summary: Where Valda grew up and works.

Morlock Hill.summary: Site of the Battle of Morlock Hill.

Mount Heroic.summary: A mountain which towers above the forests of eastern Aria.

Mugenshi Village.summary: Hidden village in the Mugenshi Gorge, led by Katsu.

Numbskull Territory.summary: Cave network lined with skulls, resembling a catacomb.

Southmaw.summary: Southmaw Asylum is a laboratory of horrors where the orphaned and discarded of the Pits beneath Metrix are subjected to nightmarish experiments.

The Flow.summary: A wild, unpredictable force of nature in Aria, the Flow shapes the landscape around it as it ebbs and flows.

The Foundry.summary: An independent radio station, one of the few in Metrix that remains free of Mendacity control.

The Golden Fields.summary: Beyond the outer walls are grand golden fields, numerous villages and towns under the protection and guidance of Solana.

The Great Gates.summary: These eight paths lead through the city to the Solarium.

The Korshem.summary: A massive tree at the heart of Aria that shelters all who live amongst its leaves.

The Maela.summary: Part of the Everfest Carnival, home to fortune tellers, seers, oracles, enchantresses, and conjurers.

The Maw.summary: Entrance to the pits, traveled by mine cage

The Oasis.summary: Water from Misteria, lava from Mt. Volcor.

The Plazas.summary: Connect the outer city sectors, a space to gather and hear news.

The Shadow Crypts.summary: Dim halls within the Demonastery where a black mold containing warped microcosms of shifting light grows.

The Silvaris.summary: A series of beautiful public gardens surrounding the inner sanctum of Solana.

The Solarium.summary: The inner sanctum, home to the Light of Sol.

The Valdur.summary: Part of the Everfest Carnival, known for strongmen acts and work with animals.

The Vitiate Gateway.summary: Last of the 9 portals connecting Rathe to íArathael.

Torched Territory.summary: Marked with trigger-sensitive flamethrowers, fire traps, lava pits, and plenty of explosives.

Trōpal-Dhani.summary: A legendary city said to have survived the Dhani empire's collapse, hiding its lost magic and treasures in the most perilous ruins.

Yvors Peak.summary: Statue of Yvor located here, doorway to an armory.

iArathael.summary: Separate plane accessed via a portal in the Demonastery, home of the Old Ones.

Dregs.summary: Humanoid figures with bloated, rotting bodies; their faces a mass of melted, discoloured skin, dripping the length of their bony, twisted limbs.

Glutgorr.summary: Mountain of Meat. A giant grown from a multitude of willing bodies. A singular aberrant baby born from a thousand mothers.

Puppeteer.summary: A repulsive creature emerged through the opening, resembling a mass of human bodies held together by some monstrous cancer, an enormous eye in its center above a slavering mouth.

Shadowrealm Walker.summary: Huge stilt-legged predators of i'Arathael, slow to anger, resembling praying mantises.

Ankis.summary: The crystalline creature does not bleed. It shatters. Its teeth are harder than stone, with serrated edges and a needle-like tip to tear through flesh. Its limbs are long and thin, with sharp points to allow it to grip onto most surfaces, and scale the difficult terrain of the Savage Lands.

Apophis.summary: A large serpent with barbed scales, which can be found lurking within larger bodies of magma, storing energy and lying in wait. These creatures move with Volcor's lava flows, moving through the magma to feast on those caught in the flow's path. While they can occasionally be found on land, they move much more slowly, and are thus vulnerable to attack.

Azeri.summary: The Azeri are elusive and mysterious, lingering in the innermost depths of the Savage Lands.

Brawnhide.summary: A giant, furred beast with long, thick canines, and small, dark eyes.

Cursed Dhani Warriors.summary: Scorpion tails, claws and human head.

Desert Fox.summary: The creature's mouth warped by two large boar-like tusks.

Fianna.summary: Tall creature, with long flowing tails, tough skin, and massive antlers crowning the top of their head.

Flare Deer.summary: Its scent glands swollen with massive oozing growths.

Hydra.summary: A sea creature with boat-sized flippers, three heads and perhaps as large as three whales, end on end.

Longma.summary: Despite their vague resemblance, longma are larger than the mounts used by Solana, ink-black in colour and covered in a dense coat of fur that helps to protect them from embers. Longma store heat within their bodies as a source of energy, smoke escaping their nostrils with every exhale. These hardy creatures are excellent for long-distance travel, able to withstand the heat of Volcor's landscape.

Meep.summary: Tiny, mischievous creatures recognised by their long limbs and tails, and colourful feather crests.

Morrows.summary: These tiny wisps are artificially created by the wizards of Volcor; puffs of smoke brought to life by a breath of aether. Once formed, they subsist entirely on embers, flitting to and fro amongst the fiery landscape.

Nashari.summary: A large beast with a crystalline hide, the na'shari has skin harder than most forms of metal. In spite of its tough appearance, this creature is known for its docile and friendly nature. Its round eyes and fuzzy tail make the creature incredibly popular with children, who often flock to na'shari in hopes of playing with the creature.

Ryoki.summary: These are small creatures, similar in appearance to fish, that inhabit the lava streams and rivers of Volcor. Despite the immense heat, these creatures thrive in the extreme conditions, lurking beneath the glowing surface of the magma. While their scales are almost black, their 'fins' catch alight when they break the surface of the lava, leaping from stream to stream.

Sailorbane Coral.summary: A predatory species, Sailorbane Coral grows from the rocky banks of channels, remaining submerged to conceal its presence.

Siren.summary: Enchanting merfolk who lure sailors with song and beauty — gifts please them, but poor offerings may cost you dearly.

Skera.summary: One of the Savage Lands' most skilled predators, almost completely nocturnal, relying on the darkness to mask their movements while stalking prey. Their four eyes allow them to hunt in the dark.

Vitreo.summary: A majestic creature with a thick mane, crowned with a series of large crystals that grow out from the top of its skull.

Vuurlin.summary: A large bird of prey that flies at a high altitude, only descending to roost or to attack vulnerable prey. When in flight, the tips of their feathers catch alight, creating flames that streak behind them as they soar through the sky. They're reliable messengers, and are often used by the royal court and the many generals of Volcor, due to the vuurlin's keen intelligence and powerful wings.

Welkin.summary: A wyvern species native to Aria. At first glance, the welkin resembles large, winged lizards. They have a leathery skin that, like the chameleon, changes color with variations in the Flow.

Jacaranda.summary: Branches like insectile limbs, each adorned with the body of a firefinch, pierced through the heart.

Absolon.summary: Dhani god of the great deep.

Aeronauts.summary: Daring sky captains who trade danger for speed and freedom among the clouds.

Alif.summary: A member of the Cintari, Kassai's close friend.

Alshoni.summary: A faction founded by a particular branch of the Royal Bloodline, brought together by blood ties, headed up by Lord Wizard Chiyo, cousin to the Emperor.

Braumeister.summary: Elite brewers of Aria making spirits which possess unique effects.

Chiyo.summary: Lord Wizard, Head of the Alshoni faction and cousin to the Emperor.

Cintari.summary: A group of bloodthirsty brigands who seek the thrill of battle.

Cogwerx.summary: Credited with inventing steam technology and revolutionising Metrix through steam compression for energy, largely responsible for the city's success and development.

DhaniEmpire.summary: A fallen civilisation once ruled by an arcane empress, whose mastery of aether ended in cataclysmic ruin.

Dracai.summary: Bestowed names free of restrictions or stigma, the Dracai are granted positions in the Royal Court of Volcor by the Emperor.

Ezu.summary: A faction headed up by Chancellor Yama.

Fayyad.summary: A member of the Cintari, Kassai's close friend.

FiddlersGreen.summary: An imaginary place where every immortal's whim shall be tended to by demure merfolk.

Fireseer.summary: One of the most powerful positions in the royal court of Volcor. The Fireseer is both spymaster and prophet, using Firesight to look through the empire's flames as windows across the land.

GoldkissRum.summary: Bright with a rich golden flavour, the most popular drink served at the Kraken's Barrel.

GrandMagister.summary: Serves the city of Solana as the highest representation of Sol.

HandOfSol.summary: Solana's order of knights, who defend the city from outside threats.

Kayat.summary: An illusionist, boss of the Dust Runners.

Kuraghan.summary: A fanatical pirate cult formed from reconditioned convicts. The Kuraghan worship the sea god Absolon and believe dying in battle earns them an afterlife of endless pleasure.

Mendacity.summary: A corporation that maintains control of the media, funding almost every radio station and newspaper in the city.

Min.summary: Fai's mother, Min of the Forest of Flames.

Rosetta.summary: An Order of powerful spell weavers that once stood alongside the likes of the Ollin and the Seers.

Sada.summary: Kassai's sorceress friend and member of the Cintari.

Sayashi.summary: Special force of loyal ninjas dedicated to hunting those responsible for the Emperor's death.

SuperSlam.summary: A deadly combat tournament where fighters battle in the Deathmatch Arena for glory and bloodsport.

Sutcliffe.summary: Pursued arcane knowledge despite lacking aether affinity, creating the Arknight project to unlock magical abilities. Betrayed by his creation, Viserai, he was killed and now exists trapped in a skull, tormented and longing for revenge.

TeklaticDynamism.summary: Energy source invented by Professor Teklovossen.

TekloIndustries.summary: A recent entrant to the steamtech industry, acclaimed for its innovative products and cutting-edge designs driven by a constantly experimenting R&D team.

TheFoundry.summary: An independent radio station, one of the few in Metrix that remains free of Mendacity control.

Volcai.summary: The more limited social class of Volcor; their names are homogeneous, ending in -ai as a mark of their Volcai origin status.

VoltarisGem.summary: A powerful artifact said to have an entire storm trapped within its crystalline walls.

Yama.summary: Lord Chancellor and Head of the Ezu faction.

ShadowrealmWalker.summary: Huge stilt-legged predators of i'Arathael, slow to anger, resembling praying mantises.

Baalghor.summary: Titanic corrupted demon whose lair in i'Arathael may harbor secrets of the Old Ones.

Kien.summary: A Disciple of Pain who volunteered as a blood sacrifice for Chane during an expedition into i'Arathael.

Ursur.summary: An Embra bound to Chane, drawn to the corruption of the Old Ones in the Shadowrealm.

DisciplesOfPain.summary: Chane's elite followers within the Church of Pain, accompanying him on expeditions into i'Arathael.

Runeblades.summary: Chane's Demonastery warriors who serve as guards and potential blood sacrifices on Shadowrealm expeditions.

OldOnes.summary: Ancient beings of overwhelming power from beyond Rathe who threatened to consume the world in the Third Age.

ChurchOfPain.summary: Demonastery faction led by Chane, dedicated to harnessing pain and shadow to destroy Solana.

Embra.summary: Powerful supernatural entities that feed on blood; Chane's patron Ursur is one.
